content: I'm at a crossroads with my career. I've been a physical therapist for 20 years but I'm finishing my part-time MBA in Phoenix. Should I transition into healthcare management?
That's a big decision! What's drawing you toward management versus staying in direct patient care?
I love working with athletes, especially helping them recover from injuries. But the administrative side fascinates me too. I've been handling some clinic operations on the side.
Have you considered hybrid roles that combine both? Sports medicine director positions or clinic management for athletic facilities?
That's interesting. I actually met with the director of a local sports medicine clinic last week. He said they're expanding and might need someone with both clinical and business skills.
Sounds promising! How's your MBA program going? Any particular concentration?
Healthcare administration. I'm in my final semester, just finished my capstone project on optimizing PT clinic workflows. Got an A, thankfully.
Congratulations! That's impressive while working full-time. How are you balancing everything?
It's been intense. I'm up at 5 AM most days to study before work, then I coach my daughter's archery team twice a week. My knitting group meets Thursday nights - that's my only real downtime.
You're incredibly busy! What does your husband think about the potential career shift?
Mark's been supportive but worried about the pay cut initially. We've been saving aggressively though - I've put away about $15,000 from my consulting work on the side.
That's smart planning. Have you talked to any mentors about this transition?
My old professor from PT school, Dr. Chen, actually reached out last month. She's now VP at a rehab network and said she'd mentor me if I decide to make the leap.
That's fantastic support! It sounds like you have multiple paths forward. What's your gut telling you?
